violations.text: The single doors to the outside, located near the loading dock areas, were observed with gaps and in need of weather strip replacement. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. (At the time of re-inspection, workorder observed in place for replacement of weather-stripping and brushes.) New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The Produce backroom 3 bay sink faucet diversion tube for the wall mounted chemical dispenser unit was observed to have a leaky connection below the vacuum breaker. Adjustment/repair to be made to prevent or stop the leaking water during time of use. (At the time of re-inspection, workorder for repairs was observed in place.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

At the time of inspection, prepared food items and prepackaged cheeses were observed held at 45-50 degrees Fahrenheit (Food items were observed stocked too full or above the designed cold zone). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

The single doors to the outside, located near the loading dock areas, were observed with gaps and in need of weather strip replacement. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The Produce backroom 3 bay sink faucet diversion tube for the wall mounted chemical dispenser unit was observed to have a leaky connection below the vacuum breaker. Adjustment/repair to be made to prevent or stop the leaking water during time of use. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The hand-washing sink in the produce prep area was blocked by several rolling carts. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

Hand washing aids such as paper towel and soap dispensers were observed at the mop sink in the back area. A sink used for FOOD preparation or UTENSIL washing, or a service sink or curbed cleaning facility used for the disposal of mop water or similar wastes, may not be provided with the handwashing aids and devices required for a HAND WASHING SINK. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2024

The sanitizer dispenser of the three bay sink located in the produce area did not dispense sanitizer leading to staff failure to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Staff instructed to manually pour sanitizer, mix with water, and confirm adequate concentration with test strips. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

Knives and other utensils were observed stored in unclean area between wall and knife rack. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
Single service items (located in the bakery area) are not being stored properly in their original protective package. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) The lever that dispenses cold water at the hand sink in the ware-washing area of the meat department 2) Several lights were out in the produce prep area 3) Air curtains in produce walk-in 4) Dish machine in bakery department New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) Fan covers in walk-in coolers throughout the establishment had dust build up 2) Lights in produce prep area had accumulation of biofilm 3) Knife rack in produce area had biofilm 4) Racks facing retail doors in dairy walk-in had dried milk debris 5) Honey comb vents at several display cases throughout retail area had dust and/or biofilm New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The food prep sinks in the produce prep and deli areas were lacking air gaps. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in poor repair: 1) The floor in the walk-in cooler of the seafood department had a large crack 2) The floor in the walk-in cooler of the produce department had several large cracks and pits 3) The floor to wall junctures inside of a walk-in freezer had large gaps (no longer sealed) 4) Several ceiling tiles throughout the retail areas had heavy water damamge New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

Prepackaged Olives held in the Olive Cold Hold Case were observed held at 40 to 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Olives observed held at 45 to 49 degrees Fahrenheit were removed for Voluntarily disposal at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

At the time of inspection, Bakery items (Rolls/Pastries) were observed available for customer self selection without proper ingredient information viewable by the customer. Bulk FOOD that is available for CONSUMER self-dispensing shall have the following information in plain view of the CONSUMER: (1) The manufacturer's or processor's label that was provided with the FOOD; or (2) A card, sign, or other method of notification available that includes the common name of the food, a list of ingredients/allergens. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in poor repair/not properly adjusted... - The Cooler case used for prepackaged olives was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 to 49 degrees Fahrenheit from one side to the other. At the time of inspection, the unit was taken out of service for repair/adjustment (defrosting). - A knife at the Cheese Bazar was observed to have a chipped blade end. At the time of inspection, the knife was taken out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023
